summary_text: **Findings** 1. **Current logic hard-switches from “due” to “missed” with no recovery lane.** `_find_due_post()` only considers `now-30min..now`, then `_detect_missed_posts()` marks stale approved rows as missed after 3 cycles. There is no overdue catch-up branch after daemon outage/collision. Refs: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L192), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L518) 2. **Missed demotion counter is in-memory only, so behavior is restart-sensitive and non-durable.** `_missed_cycle_count` resets on process restart and is not persisted in queue/Notion; outage + restart can produce inconsistent demotion timing. Refs: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L69), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L522) 3. **Backfill explicitly avoids past slots, so it cannot recover missed windows by design.** Backfill fills only future same-day slots (`>= now+5min`) and leaves past windows to missed detection. Refs: [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L358), [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L367) 4. **Operator-intent guard exists for manual cadence, but catch-up path is absent.** Manual/non-canonical schedule detection prevents automation from overriding curated days, which is good; missing piece is an outage-aware, operator-safe catch-up policy. Refs: [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L375) --- **Recommended implementation (safe catch-up preserving operator intent)** 1. **Add explicit overdue-catchup candidate selection before missed demotion.** In `_post_due_jobs()`: - keep current due selection first (`_find_due_post`) - if none, call new `_find_catchup_post(posts, now)` - only if that is also none, run missed demotion Refs insertion point: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L514) 2. **Define catch-up eligibility with tight boundaries.** New constants: - `CATCHUP_MAX_AGE_HOURS` (e.g. 12 or 24) - `CATCHUP_MAX_PER_CYCLE = 1` - `CATCHUP_RECENT_EDIT_COOLDOWN_MIN` (skip rows edited recently via `_schedule_changed_at` / Notion last-edited snapshot) Eligibility: - `status == approved` - `scheduled_for < now - MAX_LATENESS` - `scheduled_for >= now - CATCHUP_MAX_AGE` - not `force_post` conflict (force keeps top priority) - not in operator-managed day mode (reuse manual-cadence guard concept) 3. **Separate “overdue handled” from “missed terminal” semantics.** Do **not** demote catch-up-eligible rows to `missed`. Demote to missed only when: - older than catch-up horizon, or - exceeded durable retry budget (`_catchup_attempts` persisted in queue row) This preserves operator intent and prevents blind loss after temporary daemon failure. 4. **Make counters durable (queue-local), not process-local only.** Replace pure `_missed_cycle_count` dependency with row fields: - `_catchup_attempts` - `_last_catchup_attempt_at` so restart does not reset policy behavior. 5. **Respect daily cap while still allowing controlled recovery.** Catch-up should obey `MAX_DAILY_POSTS`; if cap reached, keep row `approved` and retry next cycle/day (not immediate missed). --- **Tests to add/update** 1. **Catch-up selection path** - Given overdue approved row within catch-up horizon and no due row, scheduler picks and posts it. Target file: [test_scheduler_r11_r12.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py) 2. **No blind missed for catch-up-eligible rows** - Overdue row inside horizon is not marked missed when catch-up lane is available. 3. **Terminal demotion only after horizon/budget** - Row older than `CATCHUP_MAX_AGE_HOURS` (or attempts exceeded) is marked missed. 4. **Operator-managed schedule protection** - If today has non-canonical operator schedule, catch-up automation does not reshuffle/override that cadence. 5. **Restart durability** - Simulate restart: persisted `_catchup_attempts` continues correctly (no reset to unsafe behavior). 6. **Daily cap interact...
